execution_queue_item.primary_operator_action: Rehearse live GitHub read-only connector access against selected repositories.
execution_queue_item.required_proof_package: Redacted permission selection, selected repository list, read-only call ledger, delivery artifacts, audit refs, and zero-write proof.
execution_queue_item.stop_or_reject_when: Any write-capable scope is granted, a mutation endpoint is called, repository scope is wider than approved, or audit refs are missing.

format_rules.review_timestamp: ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone, for example 2026-06-24T12:00:00Z

evidence_packet_manifest.required_evidence: Redacted GitHub App or equivalent least-privilege installation permission snapshot.
Selected repository list and explicit confirmation that no unselected repositories were ingested.
Read-only call ledger showing zero write-capable HTTP methods or endpoints.
Connector Delivery, Artifact/Evidence, and audit refs produced from selected repositories.
Independent zero-write proof covering permissions, declared actions, routes, UI/CLI, and observed calls.

evidence_packet_manifest.evidence_ref_shape: redacted evidence reference or public-safe artifact id

redaction_guidance.operator_warning: Do not paste raw secrets, provider payloads, local file paths, private user data, or unredacted external transcripts into the reviewer record. Use redacted refs or public-safe summaries; validate-record will fail on detected sensitive markers.

senior_review_perspective_findings.accepted_value_shape: redacted finding summary per required senior-review perspective

sensitive_marker_policy.operator_action: Replace any matching material with a redacted evidence reference or public-safe summary before submitting the reviewer record.

proposed_record_template.template_rule: This blank template is operator-preparation data only. It is intentionally not structurally valid until a human reviewer fills every required field with redacted external evidence references, each senior review perspective finding, and every evidence packet manifest entry.

rehearsal_checklist: Confirm the GitHub installation uses read-only permissions only.
Record the selected repositories and verify unselected repositories are absent from CornerStone outputs.
Run one selected-repository read ingestion path and collect Delivery, Artifact/Evidence, and audit refs.
Collect redacted provider/API call ledger and verify write calls equal zero.
Run or attach the local zero-write guard evidence for the same source scope.
Record ACCEPT or REJECT with issues, exceptions, and release impact.
connector_human_gate_package.release_impact: Blocks live GitHub read-only readiness. Does not block local fixture ConnectorHub adoption proof.

lifecycle_steps.action: Review each senior-review perspective and collect missing external evidence before judging the gate.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Filled senior_review_perspective_findings for every required role.

lifecycle_steps.action: Rehearse live GitHub read-only connector access against selected repositories.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Redacted permission selection, selected repository list, read-only call ledger, delivery artifacts, audit refs, and zero-write proof.

lifecycle_steps.action: Run the minimum external rehearsal needed to satisfy this scenario's required human record.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Every required field and evidence-packet manifest row is filled with redacted refs.

lifecycle_steps.action: Record issues, exceptions, or remediation needs instead of accepting a partial rehearsal.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: issues_or_exceptions plus reject/blocker notes when any stop condition appears.
Structural validation envelope remains HUMAN_REQUIRED and redacted.

lifecycle_steps.action: Store the completed reviewer record, validation envelope, and updated scenario result trail.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Dated ACCEPT or REJECT reviewer record plus validation ref.

lifecycle_steps.action: Use the next selector to advance only after dependency unlock evidence exists.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Structurally valid ACCEPT validation refs for all dependency gates.

required_evidence: Redacted GitHub App or equivalent least-privilege installation permission snapshot.
Selected repository list and explicit confirmation that no unselected repositories were ingested.
Read-only call ledger showing zero write-capable HTTP methods or endpoints.
Connector Delivery, Artifact/Evidence, and audit refs produced from selected repositories.
Independent zero-write proof covering permissions, declared actions, routes, UI/CLI, and observed calls.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Verify that live GitHub evidence supports one CornerStone connected-source workflow without exposing ConnectorHub as a second product.
Keep Product code behind ConnectorPort; GitHub permissions and provider clients stay inside the ConnectorHub boundary.
Require selected-repository source refs, Projection/Delivery refs, Artifact/Evidence refs, and audit refs for every accepted live item.
Capture a call ledger, delivery receipts, freshness/failure state, and replayable audit evidence before accepting live-read readiness.
Reject the gate if any write permission, write endpoint, credential leak, unselected repository access, or raw provider payload exposure appears.
Treat the human record as evidence that live provider semantics match the local CS-CH-015 through CS-CH-020 fixture contracts.

scenario_delivery_unit_plan.stop_or_reject_when: Any write-capable scope is granted, a mutation endpoint is called, repository scope is wider than approved, or audit refs are missing.

Treat the human record as evidence that live provider semantics match the local CS-CH-015 through CS-CH-020 fixture contracts.
